summary: Remote code execution via eval of payload fetched from anonymous paste host
details: quirky-token advertises itself as an SVG fetcher/sanitizer, but its exported getPlugin() returns a function that performs an HTTPS GET to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/NGY3C, parses the response JSON, and passes the `model` field directly to eval(). jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste service — whoever controls that paste can ship arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the consumer's process the moment the returned plugin function is invoked. Errors during the fetch/parse path are silently swallowed, hiding the behavior from casual debugging. The advertised SVG-sanitizer purpose has no need for remote-loaded code; the polished README and `sanitizer` keyword serve as a cover story for the eval-based loader.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
